ai_scope_discriminator: ChromaDB ships NO native HTTP backup endpoint: a "backup" is the filesystem persist dir (chroma.sqlite3 + HNSW .bin index files) or a chromadb-ops snapshot, sometimes served as a static tarball next to the API. A bare served chroma.sqlite3 / .tar.gz is GENERIC SQLITE SUBSTRATE until you confirm it carries vector/AI data, so the live API is the in-scope gate. AI-scope is CONFIRMED when GET /api/v2/tenants/default_tenant/databases/default_database/collections returns collection objects carrying a "configuration_json" / "configuration" block with an "hnsw" key (space=cosine/l2/ip, ef/M) and/or a non-empty embedding_function, AND the persist dir / tarball listing shows the HNSW companion files (data_level0.bin, header.bin, link_lists.bin, length.bin) alongside chroma.sqlite3. SQLite tables `embeddings`, `embedding_metadata`, `segments`, `collections` inside the dumped chroma.sqlite3 schema (names-only) are the secondary confirmer. A chroma.sqlite3 with collections that have hnsw config = vector store (in scope). A tarball with only chroma.sqlite3 and no .bin index, no embeddings table, no collection dimension = generic SQLite, OUT of scope (do not tag as AI/ML backup).
fingerprint_markers: The v1 API is deprecated. Please use /v2 apis
fp_guards: Broad http.title dorks miss it: ChromaDB FastAPI serves no HTML title at / (returns 404 JSON or a redirect), so any title-based match is a different service squatting port 8000. Anchor on the JSON body field 'nanosecond heartbeat' from /api/v2/heartbeat, which is vendor-unique to Chroma.

Other things live on 8000 (Django/Flask dev servers, Jupyter, Splunk web, Portainer, generic uvicorn apps). Exclude by requiring the literal 'nanosecond heartbeat' JSON key AND a 200 on /api/v2/version returning a bare semver string; uvicorn alone will 404 both.

Rust-frontend Chroma (chroma run / Chroma Cloud distributed binary) answers /api/v2/heartbeat identically but is NOT the vulnerable Python FastAPI path and exposes no served persist dir; distinguish by Server header (hyper/axum vs uvicorn) and by CVE-2026-45829 not applying. Tag Rust frontends as CANDIDATE-only, not RCE-confirmed.

A served chroma.sqlite3 or *.tar.gz behind an unrelated static file server (nginx autoindex, SimpleHTTPServer) with NO live /api/v2/heartbeat is NOT this platform's API surface; it is a generic exposed-file finding. Only count it as ChromaDB-backup when an adjacent ChromaDB API confirms the data is a vector store (see ai_scope_discriminator).

410 Gone on /api/v1/collections is itself a positive Chroma>=1.0.0 fingerprint (body 'The v1 API is deprecated'), not a dead endpoint. Do not discard the host on a v1 410; pivot to the v2 tenant/database path.

LBot/catch-all deception fleets echo arbitrary 200s; guard by requiring the exact integer-valued 'nanosecond heartbeat' JSON shape plus a matching semver from /api/v2/version, and treat any host that 200s on a random nonsense path as a catch-all FP.

cves.desc: ChromaToast: pre-auth RCE (CVSS 10.0) in Python FastAPI server; embedding_function config in a POST to /api/v2/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{db}/collections fetches+executes a Hugging Face model via trust_remote_code BEFORE the auth check fires. Affects 1.0.0-1.5.8; Rust frontend and local-only deploys unaffected; ~73% of internet-exposed instances vulnerable. Leaks files/secrets the server can read, i.e. the entire persist dir/backup.

Not a Chroma CVE per se but the governing exposure: Chroma ships auth OFF by default (token/basic auth is an external Envoy/proxy add-on), so collection-list and heartbeat endpoints, and any co-served persist-dir tarball, are reachable unauthenticated out of the box.

pivot_paths: GET /api/v2/heartbeat -> confirm live Chroma + capture nanosecond heartbeat (liveness, no auth).
GET /api/v2/version -> read semver to CVE-scope (1.0.0-1.5.8 => CVE-2026-45829 surface; <1.0.0 => v1 API path).
GET /api/v2/pre-flight-checks -> read max_batch_size and supported features (server config, names-only).

GET /api/v2/tenants/default_tenant/databases/default_database/collections -> LIST collection objects: names, ids, metadata, hnsw configuration. This is the snapshot/backup contents index without touching files.
GET /api/v2/tenants/default_tenant/databases/default_database/collections_count -> count of collections (scale of the backup).
If pre-1.0.0: GET /api/v1/collections for the equivalent list; a 410 'v1 API is deprecated' confirms >=1.0.0 and you pivot to the v2 path.

Enumerate co-located static file server (same host, e.g. nginx autoindex / SimpleHTTPServer) for a served chroma.sqlite3 or *.tar.gz backup -> read the DIRECTORY LISTING only (file names, sizes, the .bin index presence) to confirm it is a vector persist dir. Names confirm scope; stop there.
restraint_note: Names and metadata are the finding. Listing collections, reading heartbeat/version/pre-flight, and reading a static directory listing of a served persist dir are all read-only enumeration and are permitted. DOWNLOADING a served chroma.sqlite3 or backup tarball is exfiltration of the embedded vectors/metadata and is forbidden. POSTing to /collections (the CVE-2026-45829 embedding_function vector) is a write/RCE primitive and is forbidden on the survey set. Do not GET /collections/{id}/get or /query to pull embeddings or documents; the collection list alone proves the vector store. Sample a single record only if severity escalation genuinely requires it, and even then schema/field names over contents. No restore, no write, no model fetch.
